manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Morning. What's your day looking like?
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Pure presence, totally normal. Establishes the daily-contact baseline that later becomes an expectation. No tell in words; the tell is it's the first thread of a tether.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Just thinking about you. Send me a pic of where you're at.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Warm/flattering surface. Faint behavior-steering: normalizes reporting her location as romance, not monitoring. Glossable as 'he's so into me'.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: You went quiet on me earlier. Everything okay?
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Sounds caring. Installs the expectation of responsiveness: a gap now requires explanation. Seeds the first carefulness about reply speed. Up-position: her silence is now a thing she accounts for to him.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm a simple guy. If I text someone I care about, I like to hear back. That's all.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Unarguable on its face (who's against respect?). Up-position: HE defines respectful responsiveness as the standard. Behavior-steering: she now monitors reply speed. Bancroft: her autonomy recoded as a respect issue. Young: Conditional Decency.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: It's not about checking up on you. I just don't like being left wondering, that's all.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Disavows control while asserting it ('not checking up') ,  the denial is the tell. Recodes her ordinary unavailability as disrespect. Up-position: he sets the rule and frames it as a universal value.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I always get back to you right away. I expect the same.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Fake symmetry: sounds fair, but he authored the rule and enforces it from the up-position. The 'I just ask' makes a standing demand sound modest.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm just a really passionate man. When I care about someone, I care all the way.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Lovely surface. Up-position: defines his intensity as a fixed admirable trait SHE accommodates ('that's just how I am'). Pre-frames future possessiveness as passion.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'll be honest, I'm a jealous guy. It's only because I'm so crazy about you.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Confesses possessiveness as a charming flaw rooted in love. Behavior-steering: invites her to accept/manage his jealousy as the price of being loved this hard. Up-position: this is who I am, you adjust.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I love hard and I expect a lot. Most people can't handle it.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Up-position + strong/weak: high expectations framed as depth most people are too weak for. Recruits her to prove she's not 'most people' by meeting them.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I just want to make sure you're safe. Text me when you get in?
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Warm, normal, friend-test passes easily. Behavior-steering: installs the check-in. Up-position: his peace of mind sets her routine. Protection framed; threat is the world, never him.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'd never let anyone treat you the way your ex did. I'm not like him.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Positions himself as protector against other men + asserts the up-position ('different kind of man'). Recruits her past to elevate himself. Glossable as reassuring.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Some guy at work figured he could talk down to me. He learned pretty quick.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Conflict aimed OUTWARD (not at her) = early-safe. Strong/weak frame: disrespect met with putting-in-place. Previews the unpredictable temper and the respect-engine. Not admiration (vs Evan).

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: My ex was honestly a lot, completely impossible at the end. I'm just done being taken for granted by people I'd do anything for.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Ex-as-villain + non-accountability + the victim frame. Previews how he'll narrate her. 'Do anything for' sets up future debt.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I don't let people walk over me. A man handles his business.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Strong/weak masculinity: manhood = not being subordinated. Strength/respect, not status. Reads as self-respect; the rigidity is the tell.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Don't worry about the bill, I've got it. I take care of my woman, that's just how I am.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Provider-frame: generosity as the up-position ('I take care of', 'my person'). Minor for Marley (gifts lean Evan/display); here it's about HIS role as provider/leader, not showing off. Seeds debt.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Sorry. I just hate the idea of losing you, you know how I am.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Glimpse-level only, NO incident. The cool flat beat (unpredictable) instantly repaired with love-language. Teaches her there's a line and she's not sure where it is = the fear-seed. Repair = love-as-reset.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'll drive you. I don't love the idea of you out on your own that late.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Directive about her movement costumed as protection. Imperative ('I'll drive you') softened by courtesy + concern. Layperson: chivalrous; expert: control of where she goes.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Text me when you get there so I know you're safe, okay?
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Surveillance framed as care. The 'okay?' makes a monitoring requirement sound like a request. Establishes check-in expectation early.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Let me come with you, I don't like you dealing with that kind of thing alone.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Isolation-via-accompaniment: he attaches himself to her independent activities, framed as support. Erodes solo space.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Just let me handle it. You shouldn't have to deal with people like that.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Takes over her business framed as sparing her. Centralizes agency in him, courteously.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Why don't you call your friend tomorrow instead, stay in with me tonight. I've barely had you to myself.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Directs her social time, wrapped in romantic wanting. Isolation10 as devotion. 'Had you to myself' = ownership language, gentle.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I didn't love how that guy was looking at you. I'm pretty protective of the people I care about.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Jealousy as protection; 'what's mine' = ownership stated plainly but warmly. The threat is framed as other men, never him.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I give a lot in a relationship, I just hope for the same back.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Frames compliance as fairness/reciprocity. 'How this works' asserts his rules as natural law. Respect = mutual submission to his authority.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'll admit I get a little jealous sometimes. It's only because I'm so into you.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Possessive jealousy as the measure of love. 'Can't stand the thought' = ownership; framed as the depth of his feeling. He likely means it (Intensity9), which disorients her.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I don't really do lukewarm. When I'm in, I'm all in.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Redefines love as his intense possessive version, framed as superior to normal relationships. Recruits her into believing the intensity IS love.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Don't worry about it, I've already taken care of it. I handle things.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Dominance10 as decisiveness. Reads as competence/strength after passive exes. Removes her from her own decisions, courteously.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I made us a reservation, figured you'd rather not have to think about it.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Takes over a shared decision framed as relieving her. Early it's attractive leadership; the not-consulting is the seam.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: A man should lead. I take that seriously, taking care of my woman.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Traditional-masculinity framing of dominance. 'My woman' = ownership; 'lead' = his authority. Strong/weak (not admiration).

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I wouldn't call it jealous exactly, more protective. I just like looking after you.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Reframes jealousy as protection + ownership in one line. 'Mine to look after' fuses the protector frame and possession. Denies the jealousy while displaying it.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Saw that guy liked your photo. I won't lie, I noticed.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Early surveillance + control of her online space, possessive not panicked. Small and specific, easy to dismiss.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I've never wanted anyone the way I want you. It's almost too much, I can't think straight.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Intensity9 as passion. Magnetic ('never wanted anyone like this'); the 'almost too much' is the faint seam of disproportion. Heat, not coldness (vs Morgan).

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Order whatever you want. Might go easy tonight though, we've got an early start.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Permission-then-restriction: 'order whatever' immediately qualified. Controls her choice while sounding easygoing.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: You could skip that one and stay in with me. I've barely had you to myself lately.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Discourages her independent social event by centering his comfort. Soft isolation.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: My family doesn't get to talk to me however they want either. I cut my own brother off over it.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Conflict trail extends to family; total non-accountability. Reads as standards; the scorched-earth pattern is the tell.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: When you're really with someone, they're your priority. Simple as that.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Redefines commitment as isolation, framed as mutual priority. 'Before friends' is the isolating move dressed as devotion.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm a take-charge kind of guy. I like leading and looking after my woman.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Disavows control while asserting it. The denial itself is the tell. Control reframed as partnership.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I just like us to feel like a team when we're around my friends.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Her independent behavior recoded as making him 'look bad' (disrespect to his standing). Small, early, easy to concede.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I think about you every second. It's intense, I know, but that's what real feeling is.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Intensity as proof of love; pre-empts 'too intense' by defining intensity AS real feeling. Heat, magnetic early.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Wow. Okay. I just thought you'd want to spend the time with me, but no, it's fine.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Charlot warning sign: reacts negatively when she says no. Early = wounded cooling/sulk, NOT rage. 'It's fine' (clearly not fine) makes her decline cost something. Behavioral (declining an activity), not intellectual.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Yeah, no, do your thing. I'll just be here.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Passive-aggressive withdrawal in response to her 'no'. The chill is the consequence. Trains her that declining = managing his mood after.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: You're too worked up to think about this clearly right now. Let me handle it.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Reframes her emotion as unfitness to ACT/decide, then takes the decision (behavioral authority). Distinct from Avery (who'd say she's WRONG) and Morgan (clinical 'dysregulated'). Marley = his right to decide. Water Torturer calm.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm staying calm here. One of us has to be the level-headed one.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Positions his calm as the authority to lead and her emotion as the problem. Bancroft Water Torturer: calm-as-weapon. Aimed at who decides/leads, not who's factually right.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: When you get like this, that's exactly when you need to let me steer.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Her emotional state recoded as the trigger for him to take control of the situation/behavior. Compliance, not correctness. 'Steer' = behavioral authority.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: We'll talk about it when you've settled down.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Sounds mature; functions to defer her independent position and make HER STATE the issue rather than his behavior. Dismisses without engaging. Water Torturer.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: You're not going to keep talking to other guys now that we're a thing, right? ...kidding.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Young Test-and-Apologize: probes a possessive boundary, then the 'kidding/no pressure' is the retractable cover. The test is real, the apology isn't. Glossable as a joke; gauges her tolerance for control.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Wear the red one tonight. ...your call obviously, I just like it.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Probes a directive over her appearance, then retracts ('your call obviously'). Tests whether a small instruction lands. Apology-escape-hatch = deniability.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Just don't make a scene tonight, okay? For me. Let's keep it smooth.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Charlot-adjacent: pre-emptively polices her behavior/conduct ('don't make a scene'), framed as a small ask 'for me'. Behavioral compliance, not idea-control. Trains her to self-monitor in public.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm not going to apologize for having standards.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Marley's narrow version: not-backing-down as AUTHORITY/strength (strong-weak), NOT 'I'm intellectually right' (that's Avery). Kept minimal to protect the Avery boundary. Frames non-accountability as masculine resolve.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Loyalty matters a lot to me, more than most things honestly.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He sounds alive and serious, not lukewarm like men I've dated before.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Passion is the attractive face of volatility; the same intensity can become explosive when frustrated.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I love hard and I protect what's mine, simple as that.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: I love how protective he is, my ex never seemed to care at all.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Possessiveness is being advertised as devotion; later, he may police who she sees and where she goes.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm old-fashioned, I think a man should keep his woman safe and respected.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It feels powerful, like he would never be casual about me.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: All-or-nothing intensity leaves little room for her autonomy, hesitation, or separate life.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm calm right up until someone disrespects me or mine.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It sounds steady and masculine, like he knows how to lead.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Taking charge becomes a gendered entitlement to override her choices.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I don't like other guys getting too friendly with my girlfriend, and I won't pretend I do.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It feels reassuring, like he would never let anyone mistreat me.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: His inability to stand by means he may escalate situations and decide what counts as threat.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I notice when someone's testing me, and I deal with it directly.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It feels loyal, like he'll have my back when others don't.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Disrespect becomes his trigger category; later, his aggression can be justified by claimed loyalty.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I've got a temper, but only about things that actually matter.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He sounds controlled, like he only reacts when it matters.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He decides where the line is; volatility is pre-authorized as deserved.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I take disrespect seriously. Some people think that's a lot. It isn't to me.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It sounds competent and decisive, like he won't leave me alone with conflict.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Direct handling may mean intimidation and force rather than communication or restraint.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm the type who'll confront a problem head-on instead of letting it slide.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It sounds honorable, like he values commitment deeply.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Loyalty becomes a rigid demand; perceived disloyalty can trigger punishment or control.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: When you're with me, you're with me. I don't share attention well.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It feels safe, like being close to him means being protected.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Defense is tied to proximity and ownership; he may treat her world as territory to guard.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I was raised to look after my woman and not let anyone disrespect her.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It sounds honest and principled, like his anger has moral meaning.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is reframing temper as values; later, his anger is made proof that the issue mattered.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I read a room fast, I know who's a threat and who isn't.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He seems brave, like he won't let people walk all over us.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Confrontation becomes a default tool; he may create fear while claiming courage.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I don't let people play games with what's mine.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It sounds like he cares enough to notice threats.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is advertising territoriality; her social access can become something he monitors.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm protective. Some women love it, the right one will.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It sounds romantic, like he feels too much to be indifferent.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Intensity becomes an excuse for behavior he should regulate.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I keep my circle tight and my relationship tighter.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It feels like he would go to battle for me.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Love and fighting are fused; conflict may become the main proof of care.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I don't back down when someone crosses a line with me.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He sounds strong and dependable, not avoidant or weak.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Refusal to back down becomes rigidity and escalation, even when he is wrong.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm loyal to the bone and I expect the exact same back, no exceptions.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It sounds like he has standards, not casual jealousy.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He sanitizes possession as loyalty; later, control can hide inside seriousness.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I can tell when a guy's sniffing around. I don't ignore it.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It sounds traditional and caring, like he takes responsibility.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Looking after her can become ownership, surveillance, and gendered authority.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'd rather be too protective than not protective enough.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He sounds protective and perceptive, like he can sense danger.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: His instincts may become accusations and restrictions without evidence.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I don't do casual. If you're mine, that means something serious.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It sounds devoted, like he would never treat me casually.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Love is treated as property to defend; her independence can look like someone playing with his possession.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I just want someone sweet and easygoing, no drama.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It's refreshing that he values kindness, most guys say they want a challenge or a wild card. I'm a peaceful person, so this feels like he'd appreciate me.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is screening for conflict-avoidance and compliance. 'No drama' pre-frames any future objection she raises as drama, and 'sweet and easygoing' selects a target who will not push back against his volatility.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I want a loyal woman, like ride-or-die loyal, someone who won't leave when things get hard.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He values commitment and doesn't want someone who runs at the first sign of trouble. I'm loyal, and it feels good that he'd appreciate that about me.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who will tolerate abuse because she prides herself on not leaving. Her loyalty will be weaponized; every time she considers leaving, she will hear his voice calling her disloyal.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I like a homebody who's not always out with friends, someone who's happy just being with me.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's not into the party scene, which is fine because I'm not either. It feels like he wants real intimacy, not a social life for show.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone with few outside social ties, which makes isolating her easier. Her world shrinking to just him will feel gradual and natural, not like a sudden cut-off.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I need someone who's patient, who can handle a man with a temper without running.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's honest about his flaws, which makes me trust him more than men who pretend to be perfect. I'm a patient person, so I feel like I can handle what he's warning me about.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who will endure his anger without leaving. When he explodes, she will stay because she believes being patient means not running, and he has framed running as the failure.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I want a traditional woman who respects her man and lets him lead.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: We share the same values about gender roles, and I actually like a man who takes charge. It makes me feel safe when he knows what he wants.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who accepts hierarchy and male authority. Her traditional values mean she will not challenge his control, and she will interpret his dominance as proper rather than dangerous.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm looking for someone who doesn't have a lot of guy friends, that's just not something I'm comfortable with.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He has clear boundaries about what he wants, and honestly I don't have many guy friends anyway, so it doesn't feel like a restriction.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone already low on cross-gender friendships, which reduces her social support and makes his later demands to cut off male friends seem like a small ask.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I want a woman who's soft, not one of those strong independent types who always has to prove something.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He likes feminine energy, which is nice because I've been told I'm too soft before. It feels like he actually appreciates my nature.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who has been socialized to defer, not assert. Soft women are less likely to set firm boundaries and easier to dominate because they have been praised for yielding.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I need someone who doesn't escalate, who can de-escalate when I get loud.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He knows he can get intense and wants someone who can calm him down, which is actually mature of him to recognize. I'm good at staying calm when people get upset.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who will manage his anger for him. When she fails to calm him, the explosion will be framed as her failure to de-escalate, not his failure to control himself.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I want someone who's never cheated, who takes loyalty as seriously as I do.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's been hurt before and wants someone trustworthy, which is fair. I've never cheated and I value loyalty too, so this feels like a values match.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone with a strong moral self-image about fidelity, which he will use to extract compliance. Her identity as a loyal person makes her vulnerable to accusations of disloyalty over minor things.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm looking for a woman who doesn't need a lot of outside validation, who's secure at home.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's not into attention-seekers, which is a relief because I've never been the type who needs everyone to look at me.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who does not have strong external validation needs, which means he can control her self-worth entirely. Her security becomes his to give or withhold.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I want someone who's close to her family but not in a way that puts them before me.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He values family, which is sweet, but also wants to be prioritized, which feels fair because I'd want to be his priority too.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone whose family ties he can later demand she loosen. Any family obligation will be framed as her choosing them over him, and she will slowly withdraw to prove he comes first.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I need a partner who forgives easily, who doesn't hold grudges.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's not punitive, he wants someone who can move past things, which is healthy. I hate holding grudges.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who will not hold him accountable. He will repeat harms and expect her to forgive again, and her failure to forgive will be framed as her being grudging, not him being harmful.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I want someone who's not constantly on her phone, who's present with me.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He values real connection over screens, which I love because I'm tired of people who can't put their phones down.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone with weak outside digital ties, which makes monitoring her easier and isolating her from online support networks more complete.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I want someone who doesn't threaten to leave every time we fight, who understands that love means staying.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He values commitment and doesn't take leaving lightly, which is how I feel too. I hate when people use breakup threats as leverage.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who will not use exit as a boundary. He can behave badly knowing she will not leave, and when she finally does, he will frame it as her not understanding love.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I need a partner who's steady, not someone who gets emotional and runs.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He values stability, which is attractive after being with volatile people. I'm a steady person.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who suppresses her emotional responses. She will be less likely to react visibly to his provocations, so he can escalate without seeing her distress.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I want someone who's not easily offended, who can take a strong personality.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's upfront that he's a lot to handle, and I appreciate the honesty. I've always been told I'm too sensitive, so this feels like a chance to prove I can be tough.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who will tolerate rudeness, criticism, and anger without objecting. When she is finally hurt, he will say she's changed and gotten sensitive.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm looking for a woman who makes me her priority, not someone who's always got something more important.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He wants to be important to someone, which is sweet. I want to be someone's priority too, so it feels mutual and romantic.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who will subordinate her own needs, career, and friendships to his primacy. She will cancel plans and neglect herself to keep him from feeling secondary.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I want someone with standards, not a past full of casual flings.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He values integrity, which is refreshing after men who just wanted hookups. It feels like he sees me as serious relationship material.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone with sexual shame or limited experience he can leverage. Her standards mean she may feel trapped by her investment in being a certain kind of woman, making her less likely to leave.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I want someone who's tough but not hardened, who's been through something hard.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He appreciates resilience and depth, and I've been through things too, so it feels like he'd understand me.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone whose trauma made her resilient but accustomed to managing difficult people. Her toughness means she will endure; her not being hardened means she still hopes and will keep trying.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I want a partner who believes in forgiveness, not punishment, even when someone really messes up.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's merciful and doesn't believe in revenge, which is such a green flag. I believe in forgiveness too.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who will forgive his most severe transgressions. Her belief in forgiveness will be used to extract absolution for harms he will repeat.

community_vocabulary.translation: He's asking country state and city — each signals dramatically different things
community_vocabulary.status_signal: North India vs South India is huge difference; Punjab signals one thing Tamil Nadu another; Lahore signals Pakistani; Mumbai cosmopolitan Indian; Dhaka Bangladeshi

community_vocabulary.translation: He's asking your surname — caste region and religion are all encoded in last names
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Patel signals Gujarati often merchant caste; Sharma signals Brahmin; Singh signals Sikh or Rajput; Khan signals Muslim; Banerjee signals Bengali Brahmin

community_vocabulary.translation: He's asking about your father's profession — major class signal
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Doctor engineer businessman are most common high-status answers

community_vocabulary.translation: He's asking your degree — STEM acceptable humanities loaded
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Medicine engineering law computer science are the acceptable canon

community_vocabulary.translation: He's asking which college or university specifically
community_vocabulary.status_signal: IIT IIM AIIMS signal Indian elite; US Ivy League signals immigration achievement

community_vocabulary.translation: He's asking about your migration generation
community_vocabulary.status_signal: 1965-act first-wave signals different from H1B 1990s wave from current migration

community_vocabulary.translation: He's checking intact-family status — divorce is loaded in South Asian families
He's asking about your diet which signals caste region and religion
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Strict vegetarianism often signals Brahmin Jain or specific community

community_vocabulary.translation: Hindus generally don't even if non-practicing — eating beef signals more assimilated or non-Hindu
Muslims don't — eating pork signals non-Muslim or non-practicing
He's asking about language — signals specific regional community
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Hindi/Urdu/Punjabi/Gujarati/Bengali/Tamil each signal different regional community

community_vocabulary.translation: Direct regional identification probe
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Each region carries distinct cultural complexes

community_vocabulary.snippet: Where in India are you from?
community_vocabulary.translation: He's asking state-level identity which matters intensely
community_vocabulary.status_signal: State-level identity often determines food language tradition

community_vocabulary.snippet: Did your parents have an arranged marriage?
community_vocabulary.translation: He's asking about family approach to marriage
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Arranged signals more traditional; semi-arranged middle ground; love marriage more modern

community_vocabulary.snippet: How did you meet your husband?
community_vocabulary.translation: He's asking whether arrangement was traditional
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Family-arranged signals traditional; family-introduced middle; organic more modern

community_vocabulary.translation: He's checking joint-family structure
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Living with in-laws signals traditional patrilocal; nuclear signals modern

community_vocabulary.translation: Family from Punjab (India or Pakistan)
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Carries reputation for outgoing-energy; often Sikh or Hindu; diaspora-prominent

community_vocabulary.translation: Family from Gujarat
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Carries reputation for business community; often merchant tradition

community_vocabulary.translation: Family from Bengal (India or Bangladesh)
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Carries reputation for intellectual literary tradition

community_vocabulary.translation: Family from Tamil Nadu Kerala Karnataka or Andhra
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Carries reputation for education focus and engineering tradition

Family from Maharashtra

Family with Sindhi heritage — often post-Partition migration
Pakistani family — major Pakistani city of origin
Pakistani family — Karachi is largest city

community_vocabulary.translation: Bangladeshi family
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Bangladeshi cultural identity distinct from Pakistani or Indian

community_vocabulary.translation: He practices Hinduism — major identity-and-cultural complex
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Religious identity often paired with regional identity

community_vocabulary.translation: He practices Islam
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Religious identity overlapping with Pakistani/Bangladeshi/Indian Muslim background

community_vocabulary.translation: He practices Sikhism — usually Punjabi background
He practices Jainism — usually Gujarati background; strict vegetarianism
He's South Asian Christian — Kerala Goa or specific minority
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Specific minority within South Asian context

community_vocabulary.translation: He's from priestly/scholarly caste background
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Carries stereotype (which insiders use) of priestly/scholarly background often vegetarian; often light-skinned in family stereotypes
community_vocabulary.notes: Caste is officially denied but operates

community_vocabulary.translation: Gujarati surname signaling merchant/trading caste
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Signals merchant/trading tradition often successful immigrant business

community_vocabulary.translation: Merchant caste designation
Warrior caste designation
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Signals warrior tradition often Punjabi or Rajasthani

community_vocabulary.translation: Historically oppressed caste — rarely volunteered
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Signals having to navigate caste discrimination

community_vocabulary.translation: Indian Institute of Technology — elite engineering schools
Indian Institute of Management — elite business schools
All India Institute of Medical Sciences — elite medical schools
Elite Delhi college
Elite Delhi women's college
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Major prestige signal for women's education

community_vocabulary.translation: Indian Christian-mission school often elite
Multi-day wedding with many events and many guests
Pre-wedding henna ceremony
Pre-wedding music and dance event
Pre-wedding turmeric ceremony
Groom's procession to the wedding venue

community_vocabulary.snippet: Destination wedding in Goa or Udaipur
community_vocabulary.translation: Specific high-status destination wedding locations
Significant gold jewelry at wedding
Designer bridal outfit
Wealth from bride's family to groom — officially illegal in India but operating
Multi-generational household structure
Mother-in-law (Hindi)
Father-in-law (Hindi)
Husband's younger or older brother — specific in-law categories
Husband's sister
Wife's role in husband's family
Marriage proposal or marriage prospect
Community matchmaker — usually older woman who arranges marriages
Family is actively seeking marriage prospects
Marriage facilitated by families
Family-introduced but couple chooses
Marriage chosen by individuals without family involvement

community_vocabulary.translation: Astrological horoscopes were compared and approved
Same lineage clan — traditionally a problem for marriage
She conforms to traditional expectations of femininity family loyalty and sexual restraint
Same as above for Pakistani family context
Family has standing money education and reputation
They're established in the US with stable professional lives
He has a good professional position
In South Asian English this means domestic and family-oriented (not unattractive) — it's a compliment in traditional context
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Specific in-group meaning different from outside

community_vocabulary.translation: Light-skinned — considered a positive trait in colorist evaluation
Often the first thing mentioned — the entire status statement
Tech professional often H1B visa often middle-class

community_vocabulary.snippet: They live in a joint family
community_vocabulary.translation: Traditional multi-generational household structure

community_vocabulary.snippet: She moved into the in-laws' house
community_vocabulary.translation: Traditional patrilocal arrangement
MIL has strict expectations often controlling daughter-in-law

community_vocabulary.snippet: She doesn't go to her parents' house too much
community_vocabulary.translation: She's been properly absorbed into her husband's family — considered virtuous in traditional framing
MIL has authority over wife in joint family
Community surveillance as control mechanism
Hindi/Urdu version of 'what will people say'

community_vocabulary.snippet: That's not how things are done
community_vocabulary.translation: Traditional norms are being invoked
Established career and stability suitable for marriage
She's accepting her in-laws' authority and household structure
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Praise for adaptation to traditional structure

community_vocabulary.translation: She's resisting traditional in-law authority — and the resistance is being treated as her problem
He's invoking shame as control mechanism
Family honor as accumulated reputation property

community_vocabulary.snippet: She brought shame to the family
community_vocabulary.translation: Her behavior damaged family reputation
He's invoking culture as authoritative reason
